per_criterion.rationale: Each plan names specific pattern files and what they continue (e.g., Ulefone unboxing-to-camera-test flow, AICOOK pump-to-cup proof), and the voice/visual style explicitly continues the nonverbal product-led pattern documented across the 12 catalog analyses.

evidence.quote: patterns/08_hIgtWQqF5l0.json - UNBOXING - ULEPHONE ARMOR 11 5G WITH NIGHT VISION CAMERA, 447s, camera-feature test after unboxing

It is grounded in `UNBOXING - ULEPHONE ARMOR 11 5G WITH NIGHT VISION CAMERA`, which moves from box contents to settings and camera tests, and it keeps TechReview's nonverbal evidence style by making a lamp switch, mode label, and visible preview carry the story.
The channel's 'voice' is largely editorial restraint: hands, screen messages, ambient handling sounds, and hard cuts do the explaining.

per_criterion.rationale: The hooks are concrete and identifiable (bright green screen with car hidden, dry cup under running pump, eleven tally marks, success check beside dark lamp, flashing Return Home over stationary drone) but they describe procedural test states rather than arresting moments — you can describe them but a stranger is unlikely to urgently ask for the link.

evidence.quote: An extreme close-up fills the frame with only the Ulefone screen: a bright green monochrome preview of a toy car, with the room, lamp, and real object hidden outside the crop.
The machine display is lit and the pump runs while the white cup under the spout remains completely dry.
Screen bright. Room hidden.

per_criterion.rationale: Each named emotion in emotional_map is tied to a specific observable beat (curiosity from cropped green screen, doubt from normal-mode failure after lamp click, turn from hard cut to night-vision reveal), the transitions are motivated by what the viewer just saw, and the climactic moment is specific to THIS story rather than generic dramatic structure.

evidence.quote: Curiosity is produced by a bright green toy car on a phone screen with the room hidden outside the crop. Trust is produced by the complete box-to-camera setup sequence. Doubt is produced when normal camera mode loses the toy car after the lamp click. The turn is produced by a hard cut to the same car in green night vision, where it is readable again.
The desk lamp is switched off while normal camera mode turns the car into a dark blur; after a hard cut, the same framing appears in night-vision mode and the car is readable again.
Doubt is produced by the empty cup under a running machine. Methodical trust is produced by each visible check. Relief is produced by the red plug leaving the inlet.

per_criterion.rationale: All five resolutions force a re-reading of the opening (saved clip → live night-vision preview; broken machine → missing plug; defective vacuum → shipping film over sensor; software success → physical alignment gap; failed Return Home → pre-calibration state) — each makes the opening mean something different than it first appeared.

evidence.quote: The final frame widens from the opening screen image to show the lamp visibly off, the real toy car beside the phone, and the same green preview still live, changing the hook from possible saved clip into a live feature test.
The same button is pressed again and espresso drips into the same cup, changing the opening from a broken-product scene into a setup-mistake scene.
The robot starts from the same dock and passes the same chair leg without touching it, making the opening tally marks evidence of a missed setup detail, not a bad motor.

per_criterion.rationale: Every voice line carries specific delivery directions (pace, volume, timing relative to visible events), audio design names silence with measured durations and specific sources (lamp click, plug pop, film peel), vocal qualities vary across beats, and the audio layer carries story information the visuals alone do not (e.g., pump sound proving the machine is 'running' even when the cup is dry).

per_criterion.rationale: Surface variety is real (rugged phone, espresso machine, robot vacuum, smart switch, GPS drone, with different settings and physical proofs), but all five share the same 5-beat structural pattern (hook=visible odd state → setup → failed test → hidden-cause reveal → same-frame success) and the same flat, sparse-voice register; the session's own findings flag this and the evaluator flagged SB-8 as a relative risk.

evidence.quote: the evaluator still notes SB-4/SB-8 as relative risks because the full set uses repeated proof framing heavily

They diversify hook families while staying inside the channel's observed style: Leeco camera fault uses curiosity through an exact error message; AICOOK espresso uses a contrarian setup diagnosis; PREUP robot vacuum uses a mid-failure story hook; SwitchBot uses a value hook around one-millimeter placement; drone uses curiosity around a failed Return Home command.
The five plans use different bets while staying inside TechReview's observed language: night-vision proof, blocked fluid path, navigation failure, smart-home setup mismatch, and GPS calibration.

primary.notes: All five stories/storyboards/frames are well-formed with 5 scenes each, every scene has a non-empty prompt and allowed camera field, and pattern citations resolve to files in patterns/. Strengths: explicit pattern continuity (SB-1), motivated emotional arcs (SB-3), recontextualizing endings (SB-4), performable voice with engineered silence (SB-5), pacing aligned to creator median (SB-7). Weaknesses: hooks are procedurally specific rather than arresting (SB-2), several prompts demand fine-grained AI-renderable detail like a 1mm ruler reading and exact tally counts and legible UI text (SB-6), and the portfolio repeats the same 5-beat 'visible-odd-state → reveal hidden cause → same-frame success' structure across all five plans (SB-8 — also self-flagged in findings.md).

per_criterion.rationale: The plans trace voice, themes, surprise mechanisms, pacing, and recurring product-led character types directly to TechReview pattern data rather than generic tech-review conventions.
